checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=47942629.10 (2011-06-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=51048434.20 (2012-06-30) (+6.5% YoY) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet=79157845.59 (Q2 2011) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=91929330.50 (Q2 2012) (+16.1% YoY)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=284705944.40 (FY2010) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=321776172.03 (FY2011), only a 13.0% increase
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=5777619.03 (2010-12-31) grew to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=11026590.38 (2011-12-31) and further to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=16523811.82 (2012-06-30)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K Income Statement / Cash Flow Statement FY2011

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11 for single quarter (2011-07-01 to 2011-09-30), a large loss not mirrored by a comparable operating cash outflow in surrounding quarters

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=53692863.23 (2009-12-31); InventoryNet=64043165.59 (2010-12-31, +19.3%); InventoryNet=71029887.58 (2011-12-31, +10.9%) roughly tracking SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of +29.1% (2010) and +13.0% (2011)

GrossProfit=184092027.98 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=220472275.15 = 83.5% (2009); GrossProfit=234020657.82 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=284705944.40 = 82.2% (2010); GrossProfit=255033807.61 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=321776172.03 = 79.3% (2011) - modest margin compression, not a sharp divergence

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=647712.47 (Q1 2010); NetIncomeLoss=4002362.98 (Q2 2010); NetIncomeLoss=5085257.26 (Q3 2010); NetIncomeLoss=36869603.46 (Q4 2010) - highly uneven quarterly earnings

NetIncomeLoss=1404369.22 (Q1 2011); NetIncomeLoss=3202842.90 (Q2 2011); NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11 (Q3 2011); NetIncomeLoss=-5974671.26 (Q4 2011) - large swing to loss, indicating volatile (not smoothed) earnings

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2478333.64 (2009-12-31) declined to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1531768.55 (2010-12-31) even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 34803829.32 (2009-12-31) to 44268884.95 (2010-12-31)
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12459535.15 (2011-12-31) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew from 284705944.40 (2010) to 321776172.03 (2011)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Cluster of four 8-K filings in rapid succession: 8-K filing_date=2011-09-21, 8-K filing_date=2011-09-27, 8-K filing_date=2011-09-28, 8-K filing_date=2011-09-29, coinciding with a large Q3 2011 net loss (NetIncomeLoss=-40215324.11)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Series span from AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=34803829.32 (2009-12-31) through AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=51048434.20 (2012-06-30) with quarterly granularity across most concepts (Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, InventoryNet, Goodwill, etc.)
AccountsPayableCurrent and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent only have two data points each (2010-12-31, 2011-12-31), limiting trend depth for those specific line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of bad-debt allowance/reserve relative to a growing receivables base, which reduces bad debt expense and inflates reported net income and net receivables carrying value.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2478333.64 (2009-12-31) fell to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1531768.55 (2010-12-31) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent rose from 34803829.32 (2009-12-31) to 44268884.95 (2010-12-31)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-accrual of operating expenses/liabilities in a period of revenue growth, which would understate current liabilities and overstate net income for FY2011.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=15471874.38 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12459535.15 (2011-12-31) despite SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth from 284705944.40 (2010) to 321776172.03 (2011)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid acquisition-driven growth in goodwill and other non-current 'soft' assets outpacing revenue growth raises risk that these assets are carried above recoverable value pending impairment testing, and/or that acquisition accounting (purchase price allocation) overstated intangible/goodwill balances relative to tangible operating growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill=61360040.50 (2010-12-31) increased to Goodwill=94864277.62 (2011-12-31), +54.6%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only 13.0% (321776172.03 in 2011 vs 284705944.40 in 2010)
Assets=477467079.88 (2010-12-31) jumped to Assets=757802751.10 (2011-06-30) before settling at Assets=668883378.54 (2011-12-31), consistent with a large mid-2011 acquisition

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts declined sharply (2478333.64 in 2009 to 1531768.55 in 2010) even as receivables grew, suggesting possible reserve understatement that could inflate earnings
Accrued liabilities fell (15471874.38 to 12459535.15) from FY2010 to FY2011 despite 13% revenue growth, a potential under-accrual signal

Large, volatile swings in quarterly net income (e.g., +36.9M in Q4 2010, -40.2M in Q3 2011) coincide with a major mid-2011 acquisition (Goodwill nearly doubling, Assets spiking to 757.8M at 2011-06-30) and a cluster of 8-K filings in Sept 2011, plus a 10-K/A amendment in 2010 - warranting scrutiny of purchase accounting, impairment testing, and one-time charge classification
2011 net loss (-41.6M) diverged sharply from strongly positive operating cash flow (+37.5M), indicating large non-cash charges that need verification for appropriateness and timing
